title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Following the disappearance of a diamond, Mr. Bunce and Mr. Bontz attempt to search Eve (also referred to as Ivy or Yvonne) for the stolen gem. Mrs. Maloway and Mrs. Rathbone conduct a thorough search of Eve behind a screen, but find nothing. During the search, Evan Llewellyn opens a window and purchases a newspaper from a newsboy passing below. The men also search the room, including a round table and the chairs where Eve had been sitting, but the diamond remains missing.

Eve eventually reveals her plan: she had previously replaced a fake diamond on her handbag clasp with the real diamond using rubber cement. She intended to hide the real stone in the empty slot on her bag, but when she attempted to retrieve it from her small black evening clutch, she discovered the diamond was gone. Despite a secondary search of the room, the diamond cannot be found. Sir George, suspecting a thief is among the group, proposes that everyone be searched. The group agrees, and the eight individuals undergo searches, yet the "Morning Star" diamond remains missing.

In a subsequent scene, Evan Llewellyn meets with Mr. Parker Paine to discuss the incident. Evan explains that Mr. Bunce had locked the door to keep servants out, meaning the diamond must have been taken by someone in the room. Evan expresses concern that his action of buying a newspaper might make it appear as though he threw the diamond to a co-conspirator outside, but Mr. Parker Paine suggests there may be other ways the gem left the room.
